content: Hey there! I'm interested in trying out the RFL 9p driver and I found a PPT on Kangrejos 2022, but I noticed that it doesn't provide specific instructions in the demo section. I wonder if there are any documents or illustrations available on how to use the 9p driver?

Hongyu Li said:

Hey there! I'm interested in trying out the RFL 9p driver and I found a PPT on Kangrejos 2022, but I noticed that it doesn't provide specific instructions in the demo section. I wonder if there are any documents or illustrations available on how to use the 9p driver?

Hey Hongyu,

The code is here: https://github.com/wedsonaf/linux/tree/9p

It doesn't have a lot of configuration options yet, so there isn't much to be said about running it, other than, if you load it, it'll listen on port 564 (the default 9p port) and share your root file system.

I haven't had time to spend on it since September of last year, but if you're interested in working on it, let me know :)

Thanks for helping!

I'm currently trying to use the RFL 9p server driver by booting the kernel with QEMU. I was able to successfully mount the 9p server file system using the mount command: mount -t 9p 0.0.0.0 -o trans=tcp,port=564 /mnt/9. However, I noticed that the root and dev directories are empty and I'm unable to write any files to them. After looking through the 9kpd code, I found that the READ_ONLY flag is set when the 9p server opens the / directory, and it seems like it cannot be changed. I'm wondering where the / directory is located and if it's possible to change it to a specific directory that I want to use.

I'm really interested in working on this and would be thrilled to participate.

For reference, here's the QEMU command that I'm currently using: qemu-system-aarch64 -nographic -smp 1 -m 4096 -kernel arch/arm64/boot/Image -drive if=none,file=./2022-01-28-raspios-bullseye-arm64.img,id=hd0,format=raw -machine type=virt -cpu cortex-a57 -append "root=/dev/vda2 panic=1 rootfstype=ext4 rw" -device virtio-blk-device,drive=hd0 -net user,hostfwd=::2222-:22 -net nic.
I understand why I can only see the root and dev directories. I insmod the k9pd as a module after the kernel booting and see the root file system.
